description_long: This is a classic wood-handled axe featuring a robust, slightly curved handle made from a medium-brown wood, designed for ergonomic grip and efficient swinging. The axe head is forged from dark metal, with a broad, wedge-shaped blade on one side and a flat poll on the opposite end, both finished in a matte black. The blade is securely mounted to the handle, with a red-painted collar or cheek area that adds a visual accent and may provide extra durability. The cutting edge is straight and sharp, suitable for chopping wood or similar tasks. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative engravings or branding. This tool is typical of those found in urban garages, maintenance sheds, or park service facilities.
description: A wood-handled axe with a black metal blade and a red collar, featuring a curved ergonomic handle and a broad, sharp cutting edge.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the curved wooden handle and the black metal blade with a red collar.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the narrow profile of the blade and the straight alignment of the handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the other face of the blade and the handle's curvature.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the poll of the axe head and the handle's straight alignment.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with an ergonomic grip, oval cross-section, smooth finish, and no additional grip features.
attribute_blade_design: Single broad wedge-shaped blade with a straight cutting edge, flat poll, and a red-painted cheek; no beard or decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the blade fitted onto the handle and secured, likely with a wedge inside the eye.
